summary: Unit tests (labeler.test.ts) comprehensively cover all glob patterns, label assignment, priority ordering, and error handling with clear scenarios. However, workflow tests (workflow.test.ts) have critical design flaws: they depend on .github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml and rules.json, neither of which are provided in the implementation code. The test will fail at readFileSync without the workflow file, and the expect(existsSync(rules.json)) check will fail without that fixture. The workflow test also assumes specific rule definitions (with hardcoded priorities and exclude patterns) without documenting the required rules.json structure anywhere. Missing: explicit test for '?' wildcard (implemented but untested), tests for special characters/whitespace in paths, and validation of invalid glob patterns. The fixtures and scenario organization are thoughtful, but the hard external dependencies undermine testability and maintainability.
